It belongs to the genus Alophia, which is a group of bulbous perennials native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. intro: Alophia pulchella, also known as the desert lily or wild lilyturf, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Asphodelaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues.
